---
title: "TargetColourVsReactionTimeVisualization"
author: "Kritika"
date: "2026-03-10"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r rt_color_condition_plot, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}


# Load required libraries
# ggplot2 -> plotting
# dplyr -> data manipulation
# stringr -> string extraction
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

# -----------------------------
# Folder paths
# -----------------------------
# Define paths where participant CSV files are stored
# Separate folders exist for single-target and multiple-target lab data

lab_single_path <- "/home/kritika/MTech/Sem4/BRSM/Project/Attention Task Validation/data_brsm/single/lab"
lab_multiple_path <- "/home/kritika/MTech/Sem4/BRSM/Project/Attention Task Validation/data_brsm/multiple/lab"

# -----------------------------
# Get CSV files
# -----------------------------
# Retrieve all CSV files present in the folders
# full.names = TRUE returns complete file paths

single_files <- list.files(lab_single_path, pattern="*.csv", full.names=TRUE)
multiple_files <- list.files(lab_multiple_path, pattern="*.csv", full.names=TRUE)

# -----------------------------
# Safe reader
# -----------------------------
# Function to safely read participant files and extract reaction time

read_safe <- function(file){

  # Read CSV while keeping columns as character to avoid parsing issues
  df <- read.csv(file,
                 stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
                 colClasses = "character")

  # Remove rows where mouse.time is missing
  df <- df[!is.na(df$mouse.time), ]

  # Extract numeric reaction time from mouse.time column
  # Convert seconds to milliseconds
  df$IRT <- as.numeric(str_extract(df$mouse.time, "[0-9.]+")) * 1000

  return(df)
}

# -----------------------------
# Load all participant files
# -----------------------------
# Apply the safe reader to all files and combine them into one dataset

lab_single <- bind_rows(lapply(single_files, read_safe))
lab_multiple <- bind_rows(lapply(multiple_files, read_safe))

# Add condition labels for later comparison
lab_single$Condition <- "Single Target"
lab_multiple$Condition <- "Multiple Target"

# Combine both conditions into one dataset
lab_data <- bind_rows(lab_single, lab_multiple)

# -----------------------------
# Remove extreme RT values
# -----------------------------
# Filter out extremely fast or slow responses
# (<200 ms likely anticipatory responses, >5000 ms likely distractions)

lab_data <- lab_data %>%
  filter(IRT > 200 & IRT < 5000)

# -----------------------------
# Median RT by color
# -----------------------------
# Calculate median reaction time for each target color
# separately for single-target and multiple-target conditions

color_summary <- lab_data %>%
  group_by(Condition, target_col) %>%
  summarise(
    median_RT = median(IRT, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# -----------------------------
# Plot grouped bar chart
# -----------------------------
# Visualize median RT for each color across conditions

ggplot(color_summary,
       aes(x = target_col,
           y = median_RT,
           fill = Condition)) +

  geom_bar(stat = "identity",
           position = position_dodge(),
           width = 0.7) +

  labs(
    title = "Median Reaction Time by Target Color and Condition",
    x = "Target Color",
    y = "Median Reaction Time (ms)"
  ) +

  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )
```


Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
